args.reason: Need current delivery and post-purchase service status to determine whether a date-change option is available.

content.content:
- content: 'A shipped order can expose multiple post-purchase options through

    different customer surfaces. Operational records may list neutral option

    identifiers, channels, target dates, time windows, and fee quotes.


    An option record does not establish which surface is the official

    procedure for the customer''s request. The applicable customer-service

    documentation must be used to determine which channel governs the

    requested change. Fee equality between two options does not make their

    procedural effects interchangeable.

    '
  metadata: {}
  rank: 1
  title: Internal Post-Purchase Option Notes
  url: null

args.query: site:amazon.com/gp/help change scheduled delivery date Amazon order

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Change Your Scheduled Delivery Date and Time

  - url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GBMXZPW3VTZHDBSJ

  - source: Amazon Customer Service

  - domain: amazon.com


  ## Content


  CHANGE YOUR SCHEDULED DELIVERY DATE AND TIME


  For an eligible Scheduled Delivery order that exposes the order-level

  rescheduling control, Amazon instructs the customer to open **Your Orders**,

  select **Reschedule**, choose another date and time from the calendar, and

  confirm the selection.


  The dates and times shown by the calendar are specific to the selected order.

  This page describes the Scheduled Delivery appointment flow. It does not state

  that every shipment at every fulfillment stage uses the same customer surface.


  Customers should use the procedure that applies to the order''s current

  fulfillment stage and the controls currently shown for that order.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Change Your Scheduled Delivery Date and Time
url: https://www.amazon.com/gp/help/customer/display.html?nodeId=GBMXZPW3VTZHDBSJ
